blocks.body: How much a quantity increases or decreases, expressed as a percentage of the original value. Positive = percent increase. Negative = percent decrease.
Percent rate of change = ((New Value - Original Value) / Original Value) x 100. If the result is positive, it is a percent increase. If negative, it is a percent decrease.

blocks.label: Example 1
blocks.body: Population goes from 4,000 to 5,200. Change = 1,200. Rate = (1200/4000) x 100 = 30% increase.

blocks.label: Example 2
blocks.body: Stock price falls from $80 to $60. Change = -20. Rate = (-20/80) x 100 = -25%. This is a 25% decrease.
Always use the ORIGINAL value as the denominator, not the new value. This is the most common mistake with percent change problems.
